nodes.label: P2.2's 'intrinsic to forward-only locality' is OVERTURNED
nodes.description: The depth wall was intrinsic to the ENERGY OBJECTIVE (Sigma h^2), not to forward-only locality itself. With a discriminative (contrastive) objective plus cross-layer coordination, forward-only unsupervised learning composes depth given headroom. This is the resolution of the density != class through-line opened in Phase-1 exp0.
nodes.rationale: Directly revises the phase2_report_bridge_phase3 caveat; the literature pass (GIM/CLAPP existence proofs) is what caught the over-strong Phase-2 wording.

nodes.label: contrast objective (InfoNCE, two-mask views)
nodes.description: The adopted SCFF objective: InfoNCE contrastive loss over two masked views of a sample, temperature 0.5. Preserves discriminative (class-relevant) information rather than all input information (density), which is why it stays above the random-projection selectivity floor at every depth while masked-reconstruction falls below it.

hyperedges.description: The complete P3.0->P3.3 decision chain: objective family is the lever (P3.0, contrast wins selectivity) -> coordination eases decline but flat-CIFAR has no headroom (P3.1) -> given a built headroom task, contrast+coordination genuinely COMPOSES depth, overturning P2.2 (P3.2) -> the reframe re-earns and improves the continual win (P3.3) -> ADOPT.
